title: Ceropegia edwardsiae
intro: Ceropegia edwardsiae, commonly known as the 'Karwati,' is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family native to the tropical regions of Malaysia and Borneo. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ceropegia edwardsiae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ceropegia edwardsiae is often prepared in teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat various health issues.
